markdown.markdown:
My research sits at the intersection of industrial organisation, corporate governance, financial economics and econometric methods. Substantive interests include: institutional investors and corporate governance; firm strategy and market structure; econometric theory and causal inference; theories of the firm and firm objectives.

## Working Papers

**[1]** Annabel, R., A. Asher, and A. Ghosh (2022). "Company-Level GHG Emissions and an Institutional Investor's Active Ownership Objective: Long-Term Shareholder, Socially Responsible Investor, Common Owner or Stakeholder-Oriented Shareholder?" *SSRN Working Paper* 4288009. [[SSRN]](https://ssrn.com/abstract=4288009)

## PhD Thesis

Annabel, R. (2025). *Essays on Institutional Investors and Firm Behavior.* PhD thesis, School of Economics, UNSW Business School. [[DOI: 10.26190/unsworks/31326]](https://doi.org/10.26190/unsworks/31326)

**Essay 1** develops theoretical results for identifying active ownership objectives — long-term profit maximisation, common ownership, socially responsible investment, and stakeholder theory — from firm-level greenhouse gas emissions and output.

**Essay 2** develops a formal econometric framework for power-based regressions: settings where agents' relative power mediates group decisions. It provides micro-foundations, conditions for interpreting estimated coefficients, treatment effect bounds, and structural identification strategies.

**Essay 3** provides a comprehensive taxonomy of maximisation problems arising from theories of the firm and from theories of investor, manager, and stakeholder objectives, establishing the conceptual infrastructure for empirical identification of investor types.

## Presentations & Invited Talks

**2026**

- 15th Australasian Workshop on Econometrics and Health Economics, Monash Business School (Apr.)
- Microeconomic Theory Seminar, University of Sydney (May)

**2024**

- Reserve Bank of Australia and APRA, Brian Gray Scholarship programme (Feb.)

**2023**

- Econometric Society 2023 Australasia Meeting, UNSW Business School (Aug.)
- Inaugural Markets, Contracts and Organizations Conference, ANU (Jun.)
- New Frontiers in Corporate Governance: Corporate Governance with Externalities, London School of Economics (Oct., invited attendance)

**2022**

- 29th Global Finance Conference, University of Minho, Braga, Portugal (Jun.)
- 12th Financial Markets and Corporate Governance Conference, Monash Business School (Apr.)

**2021**

- 29th Colloquium on Pensions and Retirement Research, UNSW Business School (Dec.)

## Scholarships & Awards

- **Brian Gray Scholarship**, Reserve Bank of Australia and APRA (2023–2024)
- **Ryoichi Sasakawa Young Leaders Fellowship Fund Scholarship**, Nippon Foundation (2021–2024)
- **Australian Government Research Training Program (RTP) Scholarship** (2021–2024)
- **State Super Academic Scholarship** (2021)
- Nominated, UNSW Business School Award for Teaching Excellence in Tutoring (2023)
- **UNSW Co-Op Scholarship**, University of New South Wales (2013–2016)
